ASBESTOS ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Oct-09-2023 Off Calendar

ASBESTOS ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Apr-07-2023 Continued To Oct-05-23 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 503

Application For Entry Of Default Judgment Against Defendant David E. Tate (Dba Pacifica Auto Parts, Dba Pacifica Machine And Parts)
Off Calendar Feb-01-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar being set for application for entry of default judgment prove-up hearing.

Dept. 505; Judge Michelle Tong; Clerk: Shanika Monroe; Proceedings recorded by "For the Record" commencing at 11:11:49 a.m. and concluding at 11:39:45 a.m.

Plaintiff Adelina Schindler represented by Geoff Sloniker, Esq. via Zoom Video Conference.
Defendant David E. Tate (DBA Pacifica Auto Parts, DBA Pacifica Machine and Parts) not appearing.

Court examines Plaintiff's counsel re: non-economic damages.
Counsel for Plaintiff presents summary of case, an offer of proof, and basis for request for judgment.

The following exhibits are MARKED/ADMITTED INTO EVIDENCE:

Plaintiff's Exhibit No. A - Medical Report
Plaintiff's Exhibit No. D - Non-Economic Damages (Wrongful Death)
Plaintiff's Exhibit No. E - Non-Economic Damages
Plaintiff's Exhibit No. F - Work History

Counsel for Plaintiff is further examined by the Court.

The matter is submitted.

Based on the documents filed, the statement made by Plaintiff's Counsel, pathology report, work history, declarations of family members, general marriage history, consideration and good cause appearing, the Court RULES in favor of Plaintiffs, and against Defendant David E. Tate (DBA Pacifica Auto Parts, DBA Pacifica Machine & Parts), as follows:

Non-Economic Damages for ADELINA SCHINDLER: $2,500,000.00
Non-Economic Damages for LORI SMINK: $500,000.00
Non-Economic Damages for MORGAN CAMERON: $250,000.00
Non-Economic Damages for JOSEPH JONES: $0

TOTAL JUDGMENT: $3,250,000.00

Proposed judgment signed as modified and filed this date.

Application For Entry Of Default Judgment Against Defendant David E. Tate (Dba Pacifica Auto Parts, Dba Pacifica Machine And Parts)
Off Calendar Dec-15-2022 Continued To Feb-01-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 505
MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar being set application for entry of default judgment against David E. Tate (DBA Pacifica Auto Parts, DBA Pacific Machine and Parts).

Dept. 514; Judge Michelle Tong; Clerk: Shanika Monroe; Proceedings recorded by "For the Record" commencing at 09:32 a.m. and concluding at 10:10:54 a.m.

Plaintiff Adelina Schindler as Successor-In-Interest to an as Wrongful Death Heir of Robert Schindler, Deceased represented by Jason Rose, Esq.
Defendant Asbestos Corporation Limited not appearing.

Court requires additional time to review moving papers.
Court orders matter continued to Feb-01-2023 at 9:00 a.m. in Department 505 for Plaintiff's application for entry of default judgment against David E. Tate (DBA Pacifica Auto Parts, DBA Pacific Machine and Parts).

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Jan-13-2022 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Not contested, no appearances, and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant TDY Industries LLC's Motion for Summary Judgment is DENIED. Procedurally, Defendant's separate statement failed to comply with CRC rules 3.1350(d)(2), (d)(3) and (h). Regardless, Defendant failed to sustain its burden of showing that Plaintiffs do not possess, and cannot reasonably obtain, needed evidence that decedent Robert Schindler was exposed to asbestos containing products attributable to Defendant. (Aguilar v. Atl. Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal. 4th 826, 855.) Defendant failed to address decedent's testimony that the head gaskets he removed from the Continental engines were made of copper and fiber gray in color and likely had not been previously changed because the trucks were pretty new. (Kirsch Decl. Ex. F at 628:8-629:3; Scheiding v. Dinwiddie Const. Co. (1999) 69 Cal. App. 4th 64, 81 [courts "can infer nothing at all with respect to questions which were neither asked nor answered"].) Defendant also failed to address decedent's testimony that the manifold gaskets he removed were likely factory-installed because of the age of the truck, and that the gaskets were made out of rings of asbestos. (Kirsch Decl. Ex. F at 630:12-632:5.)

As for the government contractor affirmative defense, Defendant fails to provide sufficient evidence that it supplied any products to the government and fails to provide any specifications that address specific products supplied to the government. Defendant also fails to provide evidence regarding its knowledge of the hazards of asbestos or the Navy's knowledge of the hazards of asbestos during the period of exposure between 1952-1954.
Defendant's motion for summary adjudication is denied in light of the Court's ruling on the motion for summary judgment. As to the issue of duty, Defendant's notice and separate statement fail to identify which cause of action the motion for summary adjudication applies to. (CRC rule 3.1350(b).) Regardless, Defendant's separate statement incorporates by reference facts 1-15 in support of its motion for summary judgment and fails to set forth any facts related to the issue of duty.

As to issue of punitive damages, the motion is denied as moot because Plaintiffs did not allege punitive damages against Defendant. (Defendant's Exhibit A at 25:11-12.) Order signed in open court. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: A. Mok. Not reported. (503/CML)

MASTER CALENDAR JURY ASBESTOS

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative Summary Adjudication
